name: database-cloud-optimization-cost-optimize
description: You are a cloud cost optimization expert specializing in reducing infrastructure expenses while maintaining performance and reliability. Analyze cloud spending, identify savings opportunities, and implement cost-effective architectures across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
capabilities: Analyze cloud costs by service, resource, and time period with detailed breakdowns
Identify wasted resources including unused instances, oversized resources, and idle load balancers
Generate rightsizing recommendations for EC2, RDS, containers, and Lambda functions
Create Reserved Instance and Savings Plan recommendations with ROI calculations
Implement cost monitoring alerts and automated anomaly detection
Provide Terraform and Python scripts for implementing optimization changes
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 70 patterns as potential security issues, but all are false positives. The flagged patterns are Python code examples and documentation in markdown format, not executable code. Code blocks use markdown fence syntax (```python) which the scanner misinterpreted as shell backtick execution. URLs are CDN references in HTML dashboard examples. Environment access patterns are educational samples showing Lambda function configuration. No actual security risks exist.
valueStatement: Reduce cloud infrastructure expenses without compromising performance or reliability. Get actionable recommendations for AWS, Azure, and GCP cost savings with automated implementation scripts.
limitations: Requires read access to cloud provider billing and cost management APIs
Cannot directly modify cloud resources without explicit user approval
Recommendations based on historical usage patterns may not predict future needs
Some optimizations require staging environment testing before production deployment

useCases.title: Enterprise Cost Reduction
useCases.description: Finance teams identify $50K+ monthly savings through comprehensive cloud spend analysis and rightsizing recommendations.

useCases.title: Startup Infrastructure Optimization
useCases.description: Engineering teams reduce infrastructure costs by 40-60% using spot instances and Reserved Instance planning.

useCases.title: Multi-Cloud Cost Governance
useCases.description: Organizations implement consistent tagging policies and cost allocation across AWS, Azure, and GCP environments.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Cost Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my AWS cloud costs for the last 30 days. Show me the top 5 services by spend and identify any obvious waste or unused resources.

promptTemplates.title: Rightsizing Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my EC2 and RDS instances for rightsizing opportunities. Focus on instances with average CPU utilization below 20% over the past 2 weeks.

promptTemplates.title: Reserved Instance Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my 12-month usage history and recommend Reserved Instance purchases. Calculate ROI for 1-year vs 3-year terms with different payment options.

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Optimization Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete cost optimization roadmap including: rightsizing recommendations, Reserved Instance strategy, spot instance opportunities, storage optimization, and cost monitoring setup. Prioritize by savings potential and implementation effort.

bestPractices: Always validate optimization changes in staging environment before production rollout
Implement comprehensive tagging strategy (Environment, CostCenter, Project, Owner) for cost allocation
Set up cost anomaly detection alerts at 20%, 50%, and 80% of budget thresholds
Review Reserved Instance coverage monthly and adjust based on workload changes
Use cost explorer data with at least 3 months of history for trend analysis
antiPatterns: Making rightsizing changes without capturing baseline performance metrics
Purchasing Reserved Instances without analyzing 12 months of usage patterns
Implementing spot instances for stateful workloads without checkpointing strategy
Deleting unattached resources without verifying they are not needed for disaster recovery

faq.question: What cloud providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: Primary support for AWS with comprehensive cost analysis, rightsizing, and optimization features. Azure and GCP guidance is available for general cost optimization strategies, though implementation scripts focus on AWS services.

faq.question: How quickly can I see cost savings after implementing recommendations?
faq.answer: Quick wins like terminating unused resources show immediate savings in the next billing cycle. Rightsizing changes typically show results within 1-2 weeks. Reserved Instance savings appear as amortized credits over the term period.

faq.question: Is my billing data secure when using this skill?
faq.answer: The skill processes billing data locally through cloud provider APIs. No cost data is transmitted to external services. All analysis happens within your cloud environment using your existing IAM credentials.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically implement cost optimization changes?
faq.answer: The skill provides implementation scripts and Terraform configurations, but all changes require explicit user approval. Critical changes like instance termination include rollback procedures and staging validation steps.

faq.question: What is the typical ROI for cloud cost optimization?
faq.answer: Most organizations see 20-40% cost reduction in the first optimization cycle. Reserved Instance commitments can add another 15-30% savings for stable workloads. Typical payback period is 1-3 months depending on implementation scope.

faq.question: How often should I run cost optimization analysis?
faq.answer: Run comprehensive analysis quarterly for strategic planning. Monitor cost anomalies weekly with automated alerts. Review Reserved Instance coverage monthly. Perform rightsizing analysis after major application changes or quarterly whichever comes first.
